User task:

Theorem title: Harmonic functions minimize Dirichlet energy among competitors with the same boundary values

Ambient theorem statement:
Harmonic functions minimize the Dirichlet energy among competitors with the same boundary values.

Let \(\Omega \subset \mathbb{R}^d\) be a bounded domain with sufficiently regular boundary, and let \(g\) be prescribed boundary data. Suppose \(u\) is a sufficiently regular function such that:
- \(u=g\) on \(\partial\Omega\),
- and \(u\) is weakly harmonic, meaning
\[
\int_\Omega \nabla u \cdot \nabla \varphi \, dx = 0
\qquad \text{for every } \varphi \in H_0^1(\Omega).
\]

Then for every sufficiently regular \(v\) with the same boundary data \(v=g\) on \(\partial\Omega\), one has
\[
\int_\Omega |\nabla u|^2\,dx \le \int_\Omega |\nabla v|^2\,dx.
\]
Moreover,
\[
\int_\Omega |\nabla v|^2\,dx
=
\int_\Omega |\nabla u|^2\,dx
+
\int_\Omega |\nabla(v-u)|^2\,dx.
\]

Target node:

{
  "id": "n_energy_split",
  "title": "Energy decomposition using the weak harmonicity test \\(\\varphi=v-u\\)",
  "informal_statement": "For \\(w:=v-u\\), if \\(u\\) and \\(v\\) have the same boundary values, then \\(w\\in H_0^1(\\Omega)\\) and\n\\[\n\\int_\\Omega |\\nabla v|^2\\,dx\n=\n\\int_\\Omega |\\nabla u|^2\\,dx\n+\n\\int_\\Omega |\\nabla w|^2\\,dx.\n\\]",
  "informal_proof_text": "Set \\(w:=v-u\\). Since \\(u=v=g\\) on \\(\\partial\\Omega\\), we have \\(w=0\\) on \\(\\partial\\Omega\\), hence \\(w\\in H_0^1(\\Omega)\\). Also \\(\\nabla v=\\nabla(u+w)=\\nabla u+\\nabla w\\), so pointwise\n\\[\n|\\nabla v|^2=|\\nabla u+\\nabla w|^2=|\\nabla u|^2+2\\nabla u\\cdot\\nabla w+|\\nabla w|^2.\n\\]\nIntegrating gives\n\\[\n\\int_\\Omega |\\nabla v|^2\\,dx\n=\n\\int_\\Omega |\\nabla u|^2\\,dx\n+2\\int_\\Omega \\nabla u\\cdot\\nabla w\\,dx\n+\\int_\\Omega |\\nabla w|^2\\,dx.\n\\]\nBecause \\(w\\in H_0^1(\\Omega)\\), it is an admissible test function in the weak harmonicity condition for \\(u\\), so\n\\[\n\\int_\\Omega \\nabla u\\cdot\\nabla w\\,dx=0.\n\\]\nTherefore the cross term vanishes and the stated identity follows. Replacing \\(w\\) by \\(v-u\\) yields the advertised form.",
  "formalization_priority": 1,
  "formalization_rationale": "Best local formal island: it packages the concrete algebraic expansion of \\(|\\nabla(u+w)|^2\\) with the key weak-form orthogonality argument \\(\\varphi=w\\), eliminating the only nontrivial cross term."
}
